title: trisetum spicatum subsp. alaskanum
intro: Trisetum spicatum subsp. alaskanum, also known as Alaskan hair grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The Alaskan hair grass is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to harness its medicinal properties.
